codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the resource leak in filters.go rmAllFilters() by wrapping the db.RemoveAllFilters() call in a goroutine with proper panic recovery using defer error_handling.RecoverFromPanic(). The implementation follows the established blacklists.go pattern for fire-and-forget goroutines. Cache invalidation is already handled inside the RemoveAllFilters DB function via deleteCache(). The code builds successfully, passes race detection tests, and has no new lint errors.

issues.description: Unlike blacklists.go, the goroutine doesn't log errors from the DB operation because RemoveAllFilters() doesn't return an error (it logs internally). For full pattern consistency with blacklists.go, consider updating RemoveAllFilters to return an error that the goroutine can log with context.

sharedStateObservations.observation: DB functions have inconsistent error handling patterns - some return errors (AddBlacklist, RemoveBlacklist, RemoveAllBlacklist) while others don't return anything (RemoveAllFilters). This creates inconsistency when applying the goroutine error handling pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: RemoveAllBlacklist in blacklists_db.go returns error (line 1-60), while RemoveAllFilters in filters_db.go returns void (line 117). The worker noted this but correctly didn't change the DB function signature as it wasn't required by the feature specification.

summary: The fix successfully implements the requirements: (1) Error handling added to goroutine via panic recovery, (2) Cache invalidation already present in RemoveAllFilters function, (3) Pattern generally matches blacklists.go goroutine structure, (4) All tests pass with race detection. One non-blocking note: the goroutine doesn't log DB errors because RemoveAllFilters doesn't return errors, unlike the blacklists pattern - but this is acceptable as the DB function logs errors internally.
